sn9.6:1.1: I have heard that on one occasion Ven. Anuruddha was staying among the Kosalans in a forest grove.
sn9.6:1.2: Now at that time, a devatā from the retinue of the heaven of the Thirty-three named Jālinī, one of Ven. Anuruddha’s former consorts, went to him and, on arrival, addressed him with this verse:
sn9.6:2.2: to where you used to live,
sn9.6:2.3: among the Devas of the Thirty-three,
sn9.6:2.6: by deva maidens, you will shine.”
sn9.6:3.4: those beings with deva maidens as their aim.”
sn9.6:5.1: Ven. Anuruddha: “You fool, don’t you know
sn9.6:5.4: Their nature: to arise & pass away.
sn9.6:5.5: They disband as they are arising.
sn9.6:6.1: Jālinī, there is now in deva company
sn9.6:6.3: With the utter ending of wandering on in birth,
sn9.6:6.4: there is now no further becoming.”
